title: Websites can spy on user activity by analyzing SSD behavior - Help Net Security
summary_full: Websites can spy on user activity by analyzing SSD behavior  Help Net SecurityWebsites have a new way to spy on visitors: Analyzing their SSD activity  Ars TechnicaResearchers say they can spy on your browsing by measuring SSD activity through a browser API — claim FROST attack requires no permissions or user interaction to identify which apps and websites you're using  Tom's HardwareWebsites Can Now Peek at Your SSD to See What Else You're Up To  extremetech.comFROST Side-Channel Attack Turns SSD Activity Into A New Browser Privacy Nightmare  HotHardware
